title: lathyrus alpestris var. skorpili
intro: Lathyrus alpestris var. skorpili, also known as the alpine peacock's ear or Skorpil's pea, is a variety of the alpine pea plant that belongs to the Fabaceae family of flowering plants. It is a type of perennial legume that is native to the mountainous regions of the Balkan Peninsula. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including inflammation and digestive issues, due to its high content of saponins and other bioactive compounds, which possess antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties. In herbal preparations, Lathyrus alpestris var. skorpili is typically used to make teas, infusions, and extracts that can be used to treat a range of health conditions, including gastritis, diarrhea, and respiratory infections.
